entry.title: Re: check if object is locked by user ?
entry.content: To check if a SALES ORDER object is locked by a user in ABSL, you can use the IsLockedByUser method. Here’s a simple example of how you might implement it: absl Copy code // Assume 'salesOrder' is your object if (salesOrder.IsLockedByUser()) { // Handle locked record } else { // Handle unlocked record } Make sure you adapt the code to fit your specific context and parameters.

entry.title: Cost Estimate with Quantity Structure displays international freight and Duty
entry.content:
Dear friends,

In t.code CK13N-Display Cost Estimate with Quantity Structure , it is displaying  adding International Freight and Duty , which the user is saying , it is not correct. We are in ECC 6.0

How to resolve the issue. Please suggest with t.codes and where to check to resolve the issue.

Regards
Sridhar

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA APIs and Where to Find Them
entry.content:
Very Informative..

thanks

entry.title: User Billing, License Consumption for Business Users
entry.content:
Does SAP charge a subscription for "Locked" SAP Business Users in Application and User Management?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: User Billing, License Consumption for Business Users
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your query regarding the consumption of licenses / charging of locked business users.

When a business user is locked in a Productive System, they no longer be considered in the Billing Cycle - this is reserved for Active Users only.

More information is contained in the KBA below:

2659697 - How to Restrict Business Users to Consume License in ByD/C4C System

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2659697

Kind regards,

Ronan

BYD Support

entry.title: Re: How to add External Libraries to a SAP Fiori Elements...
entry.content:
@Abhi9 

Have you tried ui5-tooling-modules? (I haven't tried using it inside a Fiori elements app)

entry.title: Re: How to Clear Customer GL Reconcilation Balance
entry.content: Dear SM1970, Please see if the below solution works for you, create a dummy customer with the recon account you need to nullify and enter a document debitting the customer and crediting another gl (this can be rounding off or similar kind GL).

entry.title: Re: CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>CR_LF hex value 0d not printed in adobe form QR code
entry.content:
No, it's not that simple.

If you want 2 blank lines, there should be three newline = three "carriage returns" i.e. either 0D0A 0D0A 0D0A or 0A 0A 0A, between "310" and "92700".

entry.title: Re: how to Scroll up onclick of save button at the bottom...
entry.content:
1.

window.scrollTo({
    top: document.body.scrollHeight, // Scroll to the bottom
    behavior: 'smooth' // Smooth scrolling
});
window.scrollTo({
    top: 0, // Scroll to the top
    behavior: 'smooth' // Smooth scrolling
});

2.

var oView = this.getView(); 
var page = oView.byId("reportPage");
 var pageDomRef = page.getDomRef();
 page.scrollTo(pageDomRef.scrollHeight, 500, 500); //  Scroll to the bottom   
 page.scrollTo(0, 500, 500); // Scroll to the top

Hi Mark,

how did you solve this issue?

I'm encountering the same problem as yours in our SAP system.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension now available on the SAP Price List
entry.content: Nice post, many thanks @Nicolas_Alech for keeping us up to date!

entry.title: Re: Web client does not display standard functions in Ana...
entry.content:
Well, it's a bug.

It should be included in a note about it and a workaround to fix it while it is being resolved in future patches.

To get out of the way, I have unchecked in the web client management a few user tables that come from previous versions of the Fixed Assets Addon and the standard functions of the system have now appeared.

entry.title: Re: CSS Designs for a Stylish and Optimized SAP Analytics Cloud Experience
entry.content: Did anyone try this already? For me it somehow does not work properly in SAC.

entry.title: Transporting content in SAP Build Apps (SAP Cloud Transport Management integration)
entry.content:
Dear community,

We have recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build Apps Web & Mobile projects, allowing you to transport released project versions between subaccounts, isolating development, testing and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes.

NOTE! The instructions below only apply to SAP Build Apps Web & Mobile projects at the time of writing this blog. Versioning is not yet supported by SAP Build Apps Backend projects.

This article will walk you through the setup of Cloud Transport Management service (CTMS), Content Agent service (CAS) and required BTP Destinations to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between landscapes. Be mindful that in your organization, parts of the setup such as a central tenant with subscription to CTMS and a development tenant with subscription to SAP Build Apps might already be in place.

The following solution diagram describes a transport setup for a three-tier landscape (DEV -> TEST -> PROD). A picture is worth a thousand words, so I suggest that you take a moment to get to know the diagram before moving on to the instructions:

1. Subaccounts

There are three types of subaccounts (tenants) involved in the scenario:

  • Source: The subaccount where development takes place. SAP Build Apps projects will be editable in this tenant.
  • Target: The subaccount(s) where content is being transported to, for example testing and production subaccounts. SAP Build Apps projects will be view-only in these tenants.
  • Central: For administering content transport between the Source and Target tenants.

The exact number of subaccounts will depend on your organization's landscape needs (DEV -> PROD, DEV -> TEST -> PROD or something else).

2. Subscriptions, Instances and Service keys

On your Source (DEV) and Target (TEST/PROD) tenants, you will need to:

  • Enable Cloud Foundry (Documentation)
  • Subscribe to SAP Build Apps (Documentation) and Content Agent (Documentation)
  • Create an instance of Content Agent (Documentation)
  • Create a service key for each instance of Content Agent (Documentation).

On the Central tenant, you need to:

  • Subscribe to Cloud Transport Management (Documentation)
  • Create an instance of Cloud Transport Management (Documentation)
  • Create a service key (Documentation) or obtain one from an administrator.

If any of the options are not available, please check that your global account has the required entitlements.

3. Role collections in BTP Cockpit

You will need to set up role collections in the BTP Cockpit so that you and others can perform required actions. Below you can find links to documentation for the different services so that you can assemble the role collections according to your organization's needs.

On the Source and Target tenants, define role collections for:

  • SAP Build Lobby (RegistryAdmin role needed for admin user defining tenant type in the next step.)
  • SAP Build Apps (Documentation)
  • Content Agent Service (Documentation)

On the Central tenant, define role collections for:

  • Cloud Transport Management (Documentation)

Assign the created role collections to yourself and other users who need access.

4. SAP Build Lobby configuration

Once you have admin access (RegistryAdmin role) to SAP Build Lobby on each tenant, give the tenant the correct type (DEV, TEST, or PROD) in the Control Tower.

More detailed instructions here: Tenant Configuration (SAP Build)

5. BTP Destinations

Now all the required services are set up and you should have access to them. Let's finalize the BTP setup by creating the required destinations to transport content:

On the Source (DEV) tenant, configure the TransportManagementService destination.

NameTransportManagementService (case sensitive)
TypeHTTP
Description 
URLuri from CTMS service key details
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2ClientCredentials
Client IDuaa → clientid from CTMS service key details
Client Secretuaa → clientsecret from CTMS service key details
Token Service URL TypeDedicated
Token Service URLuaa → url from CTMS service key details + /oauth/token
Additional properties

sourceSystemId = DEV (this will be the name of Source node in CTMS)
sourceSystemId.sap.build = DEV

More information here: Create TransportManagementService Destination (Content Agent Service)

You can test out the connection by going to Content Agent user interface on the Source (DEV) tenant. If you can see a green "Connected" text in the tile for Cloud Transport Management, it means that the destination has been set up correctly. If there is an error, inspect it to figure out the source of the issue.

On the Central tenant, configure Content Agent destinations pointing to each of your Target (TEST, PROD) tenants:

NamePick names that have the tenant role in them, for example BUILD_TEST and BUILD_PROD
TypeHTTP
Description 
URLurl from CAS service key details
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2ClientCredentials
Client IDuaa → clientid from CAS service key details
Client Secretuaa → clientsecret from CAS service key details
Token Service URL TypeDedicated
Token Service URLuaa → url from CAS service key details + /oauth/token

More information here: Create Target Node Destination (Content Agent Service)

6. Set up the Transport Landscape in CTMS

Once your destination setup is finished, you can move on to set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. This is straightforward to do with the Transport Landscape Wizard. Remember to give the Source node the same name that you put in as sourceSystemId in the previous step, for example DEV.

More detailed instructions here: Using the Transport Landscape Wizard

7. Test it out

You should now be able to select "Transport" for a released SAP Build Apps Web & Mobile project version in the SAP Build Lobby and it should appear in the transport queue in CTMS.

Happy transporting! 🚚

entry.title: Re: How to Connect CAPM With Integration Suite ?
entry.content:
Hi @hp0,

Assuming your project is based on Node.js, add the following settings to package.json.

 

  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "apiName": {
        "kind": "rest",
        "credentials": {
          "url": "https://apiurl",
          "path": "/pathprefix" //optional
        },
        "[production]": {
          "credentials": {
            "destination": "destinationName",
            "path": "/pathprefix" //optional
          }
        }
      }
    }

 

Install dependencies as follows:

 

npm add @sap-cloud-sdk/http-client@3.x @sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity@3.x @sap-cloud-sdk/resilience@3.x

 

link to document

In your event handler code, call the API.

 

const api = await cds.connect.to('apiName');
            const data = {//set payload};
            const response = await api.send({
                method: 'POST',
                path: '/path/to/endpoint',
                headers: {
                    "Content-Type": "application/json"
                },
                data: data
            });

entry.title: SAP Business One Sales - Services App - How to configure in SQL
entry.content:
Hello there.

Since patch 10.0 FP2022 or similar, native Sales and Services apps for both Android and iOS can be used in SQL Server installations.

I have tried to find documentation on how to configure it or where, but I can't find anything clear about it.

It only uses the integration frameword - B1iServer entry called B1A Server and that the same certificate generated in the mobile App that we knew until now would be valid.

Does anyone have any document or link to documentation to know what needs to be parameterized or configured to be able to use them on a SQL Server system?

From what I have read, it uses the service layer or needs it to be active, but it is not clear to me what needs to be configured, or where, or how.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How SAP Security Teams Can Help Cyber Teams Towards Cyber Resilience
entry.content:
By Jay Thoden van Velzen, Technical Advisor, Office of the Chief Security Officer

Due to my role at SAP, I am connected to the cybersecurity world as well as SAP security and Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) which gives me a perspective into both worlds. I often try to build bridges between the two, as exemplified in a recent cybersecurity podcast series (part 1, part 2).

SAP security teams and their cybersecurity counterparts are not always working as well together as they could. Bringing these teams closer together benefits both. There are three special skills that SAP teams have that cybersecurity teams benefit from, when working in close alignment - especially since cybersecurity is increasingly transitioning to resilience and business impact.

SAP security teams bring a unique perspective that cybersecurity teams often struggle with. They are typically

  • Closely aligned to the business impact of a system outage
  • Understand how to live with the risk of vulnerable systems
  • Know that humans are often the best line of defense

These aforementioned skills allow SAP security teams to help cybersecurity teams to better align with business priorities and drive towards cyber resilience.

 

SAP Teams Are Closer to Business Impact

Cybersecurity focuses on protecting networks and infrastructure, identities and access management, end points and servers, vulnerabilities and malware alerts, and office applications and services. Security teams tend to have less visibility into business applications, let alone what they are used for. That removes them one step further from the critical functions to the business these applications support. In the ‘CIA Triangle’ of cybersecurity of Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability, the last hasn’t always got the same attention.

A common security joke goes that the only secure computer is one that is locked away in its own windowless room, without keyboard, mouse or monitor, not connected to any network, and turned off. The joke works because, though absurd, many in the profession lean that way.

Customers tell us all the time how critical the availability and resilience of their SAP systems are to their business operations. SAP teams know an outage or downtime in their systems stops financial transactions, causes disruptions in the supply chain, manufacturing and delivery of services. Such interruptions to the business are exactly why it is often difficult to schedule down time to apply a security patch. Being closer to the business impact of an incident gives SAP teams an advantage in balancing priorities and what is most important.

 

SAP Teams Know How To Live With Risk

Cybersecurity is managing security risks and risk-based approaches have been advocated for over a decade. However, many security professionals still fall back to 100% compliance in practice, obstructing business users in the quest towards that secure computer. Security is often still seen as a “Department of No”, rather than enable the business to do what it must, securely.

The priority of Availability in critical business systems forces SAP teams to make difficult choices. The risk of a possible security incident related to a particular vulnerability may just not weigh up against the certainty of costs and loss of business that an update requiring downtime would inflict. SAP teams must balance security risks and business impact by necessity. Sometimes the answer is not “patch quickly” but “protect with compensating controls”.

As is the case in Operational Technology (OT) Security, SAP systems have a long lifecycle that can run in decades. Migrations and upgrades are projects that require significant effort. Their operators require specialized knowledge and have their own supporting ecosystem. Users and administrators get access only through their employer, and with the roles and authorizations appropriate for their functions. Since SAP solutions aside from their own complexity reflect the organizations in which they are deployed, even with access, an adversary may still find it difficult to navigate.

This is very different from the broad access anyone has to Windows, Linux, or open-source software. These different circumstances and constraints must be considered in threat modeling and security risk prioritization. They can also open the eyes of cybersecurity teams to new approaches that can apply elsewhere.

 

SAP Teams Understand Humans Are the Best Defense

Cybersecurity teams know that increasingly adversaries log in rather than break in. There is a market for stolen credentials, and AI-assisted phishing and social engineering are rampant. That has led many organizations to adopt Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) solutions.

MFA helps but isn’t necessarily enough. An adversary can convince an employee with access to initiate a transaction through social engineering or impersonate the employee through remote control of their laptop or hijacking a browser session.

Business processes in SAP systems include approval processes. For instance, any financial transaction over a certain amount must be justified with a business reason and require approval by another person, usually a superior or a delegate. This buys time for other detections to notice malicious behavior and provides an extra pair of eyes to validate whether the transaction is legitimate or fraudulent. To avoid detection, an adversary would need to control both the requester and approver, significantly raising the bar. Even then, the transaction might trip fraud detection alerts.

Cybersecurity teams are used to technical controls, scans and logs. SAP teams extend controls directly into business processes. Having approvers with the business context who know they are subject to financial oversight and audits judge whether a large payment has gone through normal budgetary planning adds a strong layer of defense. Setting up approved recipients also requires its own process, adding yet more obstacles for an adversary to overcome.

Such process controls with human approvers are typically outside the scope of cybersecurity threat modeling and risk assessments. Access provisioning can be conditional and contextual, as in a Zero Trust Architecture. Security teams have quality gates for provisioning access, software releases or infrastructure change management, or open firewall ports. But that doesn’t stop an abuse of access a user already has if an adversary has successfully taken over the account or turned the user into an insider threat.

Partnership between cybersecurity and SAP teams allows for the alignment between cybersecurity technical and audit compliance controls, and process- and financial audit controls. A phishing attempt deploying malware is better detected by a security control, but an odd payment is more likely to be caught by a business approver, an accounting control or a financial audit. By working together, the two teams strengthen the organization’s defenses, detection capability and resilience against threats.           

 

Better Alignment With Business Priorities

Cybersecurity teams benefit from such alignment. It allows them to better align with business priorities, and get a greater understanding of the overall security risks the organization faces. If cybersecurity teams don't understand the importance of SAP systems to the operations of the organization, they are bound to prioritize other areas.

Meanwhile, the alignment benefits the SAP security team as well. They, too, have had their challenges being heard by business leaders. By approaching business leaders together with the cybersecurity team and make a joint case to address gaps, it will be easier to justify and establish appropriate business continuity and security programs and operational practices to protect what is truly critical.

Reach out to your cybersecurity colleagues. Invite them for a coffee or lunch, and start building those bridges.

 

More Information

Several previous blogs fit into the theme of building bridges between customer SAP teams, cybersecurity teams and their business leaders. You may find these of interest:

  • Cyber Resilience and Cloud Transformation: Resolving the ERP Security Hamster Wheel
  • Agile, Secure, and Compliant Business Operations Through Resilience
  • “What Will SAP Basis Administrators Do Once We Move to RISE?” – Thoughts Following SAPinsider 2024

For a cybersecurity perspective on this theme, see the Down the Security Rabbithole podcasts on the security of critical business systems (part 1, part 2).

entry.title: Unlocking Innovation with SAP Business Technology Platform: A Beginners Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is a comprehensive suite designed to transform data into actionable insights, streamline business processes, and foster innovation. It integrates various technologies such as data management, analytics, artificial intelligence, and application development into a unified environment. This blog is intended for IT professionals, business analysts, and decision-makers who are looking to leverage SAP BTP to enhance their enterprise’s digital transformation journey.

Supporting the Shift-Right Approach

SAP BTP supports the shift-right approach, which emphasizes moving customizations, data management, and AI capabilities to the platform layer rather than embedding them directly into the core ERP system. This strategy helps keep the SAP core clean, ensuring smoother upgrades and maintenance. By leveraging SAP BTP, businesses can:

Enable Customizations: SAP BTP allows for extensive customizations through its application development tools like SAP Cloud Foundry and SAP Build. These tools enable developers to create tailored solutions that meet specific business needs without altering the core ERP system.

Enhance Data Management: With SAP Datasphere and SAP HANA, businesses can manage and analyze data efficiently. These tools provide a centralized data management solution that integrates seamlessly with the core ERP, ensuring data consistency and integrity.

Leverage AI Capabilities: SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and SAP AI Services offer advanced AI and machine learning capabilities. These services can be integrated with the core ERP to automate processes and provide intelligent insights, all while keeping the core system uncluttered.

Summary of SAP BTP Components

Database and Data Management: Real-time data processing with SAP HANA. Learn more.

Analytics: Data visualization and predictive analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. Learn more.

Application Development and Integration: Build and integrate applications with SAP Cloud Foundry, SAP Kyma, and SAP Build. Learn more.

Intelligent Technologies: Automate tasks with SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and SAP AI Services. Learn more.

Integrations: Connect systems with SAP Integration Suite, SAP Event Mesh, and SAP Cloud Connectors. Learn more.

Components of SAP BTP

Database and Database management

SAP BTP offers robust database and data management capabilities, primarily through SAP HANA. This in-memory database allows for real-time data processing and analytics, enabling businesses to make swift, informed decisions. For example, a retail company can use SAP HANA to analyze customer purchasing patterns in real-time, optimizing inventory management and improving customer satisfaction.

Analytics The analytics component of SAP BTP includes tools like SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere. SAP Datasphere is a unified data service that simplifies data access and enrichment across hybrid and cloud environments. It enables data professionals to leverage SAP applications, data lakehouse platforms, event streaming, and augmented intelligence. For instance, a manufacturing firm can use SAP Analytics Cloud to track production metrics and identify bottlenecks, while SAP Datasphere can harmonize data from various sources to provide a comprehensive view of the business landscape.

Application Development and Integration SAP BTP supports application development and integration through environments like SAP Cloud Foundry and SAP Kyma. Additionally, SAP Build offers low-code and pro-code tools for developers and business experts to create and augment enterprise applications. For example, a logistics company can develop a custom app to track shipments and integrate it with their existing SAP ERP system, ensuring real-time updates and improved logistics management. SAP’s Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) further aids in developing full-stack applications with ease.

Intelligent Technologies Intelligenttechnologies within SAP BTP include machine learning, artificial intelligence, and robotic process automation (RPA). Key components are:

SAP AI Core: This service handles the execution and operations of AI assets in a standardized, scalable manner, integrating seamlessly with SAP solutions.

SAP AI Launchpad: A multitenant SaaS application that manages AI use cases across multiple instances of AI runtimes, providing a visual interface for AI lifecycle management.

SAP AI Services: These services automate and optimize corporate processes, offering reusable AI capabilities such as document information extraction and business entity recognition.

Integrations SAP BTP excels in integrating various systems and applications through:

SAP Integration Suite: An integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS) that connects on-premise and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. It accelerates innovation and automates processes with pre-built integrations and APIs.

SAP Event Mesh: A fully managed service that enables applications to communicate through asynchronous events, enhancing agility and scalability by decoupling solutions.

SAP Cloud Connectors: These connectors facilitate secure and reliable integration between on-premise systems and SAP BTP, ensuring seamless data flow and connectivity.

SAP BTP is a powerful platform that brings together various technologies to drive business innovation and efficiency. By leveraging its components, enterprises can achieve a higher level of digital transformation and stay competitive in today’s fast-paced market.

entry.title: Quedada SAP en Honest Greens Castellana
entry.content:
¡Hola miembros de la comunidad SAP en Madrid!

La Quedada SAP en Madrid se llevará a cabo el 25 de septiembre de 2024 a las 18:30 hora local en el Honest Greens de Castellana, que esta muy cerca de la nueva oficina de SAP.

La idea es juntarnos y compartir un poco sobre todo lo que es el mundo SAP. Compartir sobre los proyectos SAP en los que trabajamos y hablar de las ultimas novedades. Así que únete y celebremos la quedada inaugural de este grupo.

Por favor, haznos saber si participarás en la quedada haciendo clic en la seccion de RSVP. No tenemos una reserva como tal en el espacio pero es un lugar bastante amplio y podriamos juntar alli un par de mesas. Nota: Cada uno es responsable de comprar su propia comida o bebida si lo desea.

¡Nos vemos pronto!

-----

¡Hola SAP Community members in Madrid!

The SAP Meetup in Madrid will take place on September 25, 2024, at 18:30 local time at Honest Greens in Castellana, which is very close to the new SAP office.

The idea is to get together and share a bit about everything in the SAP world, including the SAP projects we are working on and the latest news. So join us, and let's celebrate this group's inaugural meetup.

Please let us know if you will be participating in the meetup by clicking on the RSVP section. We do not have a reservation in the space, but it is fairly large, and we could put a couple of tables together there. Note: Everyone is responsible for purchasing their own food or drinks if they wish to.

See you soon!

Antonio Maradiaga
Developer Advocate @ SAP

entry.title: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content:

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

Then my following question is that :  where i can find which message types are available for Additional Data Tab?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: AMC and PM order
entry.content: hello Very interesting, thanks for the answer

entry.title: Re: AMC and PM order
entry.content: hello... Very interesting, thanks for the answer

entry.title: SAP TRM: Separation of loan principal and interest payments
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
we are currently facing the following challenge in our public cloud system. 

Problem:
Currently, SAP TRM can only post to one account. When the account "Liabilities to Affiliated Companies" is used for payments, the interest income is also posted to this account instead of to "Receivables from Affiliated Companies." It is not possible to differentiate between payment liabilities (loans) and receivables (interest).

Current Workaround:
Manual rebooking of liabilities to receivables.

Desired Solution:

  1. Enable differentiation between loan principal and interest payments.
  2. Post the loan principal to the account "Liabilities to Affiliated Companies."
  3. Post interest payments to "Receivables from Affiliated Companies."

This would eliminate the need for manual rebooking.

Process description:
First, we create a loan using the transaction "Create IR Instrument (TM_51)" with the Product Type "55I-Interest Rate Instrument - INT" and the Transaction Type "100-Invest." The Flow Type "1100-Principal Increase" and Condition Type "1200-Nominal Interest" are selected under the Interest Structure. The Condition Type "1120-Final Repayment" is used under the Repayment Structure.

In the next step, we use the transaction "Fix, Pay and Post Flows - TBB1" to post the loan.

Questions:

Is there any way we can get the desired result, which is a separation between the loan principal posting and the interest payments, without additional manual postings? Can we trigger two separate postings from TBB1? Is there any customizing that would allow us to do this?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,

Tobias

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Elevating Internal Transfers Transitions through SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding for Internal Hire
entry.content:
Any update on query @shrutithakkar   as we are facing similar issues. The pay components are wiped out and the business rules do not work properly as expected. Moreover the effective date is also not updating properly for most of the employees.

Can someone respond on this query pls?

Thanks,

Farook

entry.title: internal server error(500) after Deploying full stack cap project using mta on cloudFoundary
entry.content:
Hello community ,i have created a full stack application  then deployed my  application on cloudfoundary using commands :                                                                                                                                                               mbt build -t gen --mtar mta.tar .                                                                                                                             cf deploy gen/mta.tar .                                                                                                                                    it has successfully   deployed my application on cloudfoundary and given me three endpoints .                         my api  endpoint is working correctly but its frontend  endpoint is giving me internal server error with error code 500.

 

this is my mta.yml file code.                                                                                                                                   

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: Full_stack
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: Full_stack-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    requires:
      - name: Full_stack-db
      - name: Full_stack-auth
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
      readiness-health-check-type: http
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm
  - name: Full_stack-db-deployer
    type: hdb
    path: gen/db
    requires:
      - name: Full_stack-db
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
  - name: Full_stack
    type: approuter.nodejs
    path: app/router
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
        group: destinations
        properties:
          forwardAuthToken: true
          name: srv-api
          url: ~{srv-url}
      - name: Full_stack-auth
      - name: Full_stack-destination-service
        group: destinations
        properties:
          forwardAuthToken: false
          name: ui5
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
    parameters:
      disk-quota: 256M
      keep-existing-routes: true
      memory: 256M
  - name: Full_stack-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: Full_stack-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - ns11project1.zip
          name: ns11project1
          target-path: resources/
  - name: ns11project1
    type: html5
    path: app/project1
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms:
        []
resources:
  - name: Full_stack-db
    type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
    parameters:
      service: hana
      service-plan: hdi-shared
  - name: Full_stack-auth
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        tenant-mode: dedicated
        xsappname: Full_stack-${org}-${space}
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application
  - name: Full_stack-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: Full_stack-html5-srv
      service-plan: app-host
  - name: Full_stack-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
                HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
                Name: Full_stack-srv-api
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: Full_stack-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

entry.title: Re: FAIL: process hdbdaemon HDB Daemon not running
entry.content:
Hi SS,

there is no disk space issues.. all disks have ample spaces.

Thanks

entry.title: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
i tried finding out the answer to this problem, but nothing helped. 
I wrote a program that is creating a .TXT file, and saving it to my local pc. 
The important part is that every row in the file MUST HAVE EXACTLY 60 characters. 
In every row there is 4 different data. 

This is the sample code from the program:

 

    
CONCATENATE ls_ed_broj-data1(4)
                ls_ed_broj-data2(4)
                ls_ed_broj-data3(25) space space
                ls_ed_broj-data4(25)  INTO ls_tab RESPECTING BLANKS.
    APPEND ls_tab TO lt_tab.
  ENDLOOP.
  IF lt_tab[] IS NOT INITIAL.

 p_file_o = 'C:\testinv\TEST_file.txt'.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
  EXPORTING
    FILENAME                        = p_file_o
  TABLES
    DATA_TAB                        = lt_tab
    .
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

 

data1 and data2 should have 4 characters.
data3 and data 4 should have 25 characters. 

However it is not fixed how many of those characters are blanks.
The problem is that for some reason BLANKS from data4 are not written in the file. 

This is the example of what is being written in the file: 
NS      5005965911                 106000071120
NS      5005965922                 106000072220
NS      5005965933                 106000073320
NS      5005965944                 106000075520


And this is the example of what should be written in the file:

NS      5005965911                 106000071120             
NS      5005965922                 106000072220             
NS      5005965933                 106000073320             
NS      5005965944                 106000075520             

 

I am not sure if here (in the post) will be visible but there should be blanks at the end of every row until the row has 60 characters. Those blanks should be the remaining characters of data4 ( in this case 12 number and 13 blanks - that ration may vary ).
The blanks are respected for data1 data2 and data3, but for some reason they are not for data4. I guess that has to be something about concatenate function and the fact thats the last data.

Can someone help me resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS extractor on NSDM_E_MCHBH (replacement of MC...
entry.content:
Hello bheemeshRao,

Thanks for the reply.

entry.title: Process Email Notification SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hi all,

Do you know if it is possible to have 3 different texts in emails for three different steps for one IBP Process.

I know that I can have an email for a step, but so far I get the same message for each step, although I have three of them in the process.

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
Hi @gerd_schoeffl,

are there any plans to provide the possibility to story builders to call this file upload via script, e.g. via a custom story button?

Thanks and best regards,

Julian

entry.title: Add standard field to Fiori App F2358
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to add some standard fields to the "monitor purchase order items" app. 
We for example want to add the incoterm. And to be precise, we are talking about fields that are not available in the "adapt filters" or "cog menu" in the app itself.

Is there any extensibility that can be done to allow this?

I dont get why SAP has chosen to not include these fields in the app when they are part of the CDS: I_PURCHASEORDERITEMAPI01

entry.title: Re: How to consume S/4HANA Cloud API using ABAP program f...
entry.content:
Hi Kaidehmann,

Let me see if I understand correctly:

1. We have a standard RAP Managed Business Object. 

2. Customer wants to make use of this RAP BO from the same ABAP instance calling on the Modify or Create operations. 

3. If the standard RAP BO has an interface implemented, that interface can be used to invoque EML operations? Is this correct?

4. What if there is no interface behavior implementation on the standard RAP object? How can we use it? We should implement a Service Consumer using the HTTP approach? 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Generative AI boosting Process Automation? Come see our the results at SAP TechEd!
entry.content:
All those sessions look great.

And after TechEd, if you want to see a longer take on AI in process automation, see the Devtoberfest session "🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation".

To see all the Devtoberfest sessions, on SAP Build and more: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI Message Mapping - Context issue
entry.content:
hi Sailaja,

PARVW--remove contexts--check equals with ZN if so pass KUNN2 (you can use collapse context .

 

hope this will help.

 

regards,

Loordh

entry.title: SCC1N Usage - Simple Way
entry.content:
SCC1N Usage

SCC1N - Client Copy through Transport

This tool should be launched in Target System/Client ONLY. It cannot be used from source/any other system.

1. Provide list of Transports to be import in proper sequence. Tool will blindly follow sequence.

2. Select Transport Type. If there are multiple types, select all types using multiple selection button.

3. If Source and Target systems are same (within same system, but different client), leave target system field. Else, select it from selection options.

4. Select Source System is it is different (ex: moving from Dev to Prod). If not, leave it blank.

5. Select 'Local Import/Export Update Time' under 'Export/Import time' option. Select 45 days before date & time (must). Do NOT select Source System date/time.

6. Select Source Client of Transport Requests (import same source client TRs only. Bundle them using excel if needed).

7. Select all Target Clients using multiple selection option.

8. Leave ALL OTHER OPTIONS BLANK without any values.

9. ENSURE ALL VALUES & DETAILS ARE PROPERLY CHECKED before execution. One wrong input / selection can ruin whole system. You can try "TEST MODE" first, in case required.

10. Import log can be found in SCC3 like below:
Finished 100 200 1 N/A 00:01:17 Multiple(6) 10.09.2024 13:30:44 XRAMA

Thank you,
Gopagani Ramakrishna
SAP Solution Architect
Hyderabad, India.

entry.title: Re: check if object is locked by user ?
entry.content:
HI 

Thank you very much for wanting to help and donating your time
The option you suggested is not available to me
I can't register
salesOrder.IsLockedByUser()

I am attaching the code with which I thought to perform the test
Maybe this will give you a direction on how I can check if an adder is locked

The problem is in the code
that when it runs in a loop and checks whether an order is locked or not
After he found some locked order
It does not continue to recheck properly
but as if treating all other orders as if they were locked
For example if order number 150 is currently locked
So all orders after this number are also considered locked


below is my code :

------------------------------------------------

var currentDate = Context.GetCurrentGlobalDateTime();
var currentDate1 = currentDate.ConvertToLocalNormalisedDateTime();
var SalesOrders = SalesOrder.QueryByElements;
var SalesOrderDate_SelParams = SalesOrders.CreateSelectionParams();
SalesOrderDate_SelParams.Add(SalesOrders.Status.ItemListCustomerOrderLifeCycleStatusCode, "I", "EQ", "1");
SalesOrderDate_SelParams.Add(SalesOrders.Status.ItemListCustomerOrderLifeCycleStatusCode, "I", "EQ", "2");
 
 
 
var SalesOrderData_Results = SalesOrders.Execute(SalesOrderDate_SelParams);
var SalesOrderData_ResultsCopy = SalesOrders.Execute(SalesOrderDate_SelParams);
 
 
foreach (var SO in SalesOrderData_ResultsCopy)
{
 
var uuid;
uuid = Library::UUID.ParseFromString(SO.UUID.content.ToString());// sales order id
var messages;
var lock;
 
lock = BOAction.CheckLock("SalesOrder", "http://sap.com/xi/AP/CRM/Global", "Root", uuid);
messages = lock.MessageTypeItem;
var validationFailed : Common:Indicator=false;
var a;
if (!lock.IsInitial() && messages.Count() > 0)
{
foreach (var message in messages)
{
 
if (message.MessageSeverityText == "E" && message.MessageID.content == "CL_CDA_DOCHD/000")
{
validationFailed = true;
a = SalesOrderData_Results.Remove(ins => ins.ID.content == SO.ID.content);
SalesOrderData_Results = a;
lock.Clear();
 
 
 
}
}
messages.Clear();
}
}
 


------------------------------------------------

I mean, actually my code is not completely correct and I probably don't check it in the right way

Anyone who can help me, I would be very happy

thanks

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
The function module GUI_UPLOAD is obsolete, you should use CL_GUI_FRONTEND_SERVICES=>GUI_UPLOAD (which is the same code behind, but whatever...)

There is the parameter TRUNC_TRAILING_BLANKS (true by default).

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Process Started but automation not triggered
entry.content: WHat do you mean your automation only has actions (so maybe does not need agent)? Every automation is run with an agent.

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content: What means "barcode scanning action", what do you want to do exactly?

entry.title: Re: New Tool to Copy Configuration Data to Clients (SCC1N)
entry.content:
Hey Friends,

It is this simple to use this new tool  which I have posted here.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/scc1n-usage-simple-way/qaq-p/13860704#M4882816

- Rama

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content: ASSIGN COMPONENT 'UNAME' OF STRUCTURE <fs_outcomp> TO <field>.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @dominik_ritter ,

for „Custom code analysis“ projects the “Scoping” feature is turned off. This project type is only for analysis. The CCM app does not support decommissioning tasks, only deletion transports during SAP S/4HANA conversion based on this "Scoping" feature. CCLM tools in SAP Solution Manager should be used for decommissioning.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

Hi @Sandra_Rossi ,

I would like to create custom fiori app which include barcode scanner button using ABAP.

So, i want to know is it possible to create.

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: check if object is locked by user ?
entry.content: HI Thank you very much for wanting to help and donating your time The option you suggested is not available to me I can't register salesOrder.IsLockedByUser() I am attaching the code with which I thought to perform the test Maybe this will give you a direction on how I can check if an adder is locked The problem is in the code that when it runs in a loop and checks whether an order is locked or not After he found some locked order It does not continue to recheck properly but as if treating all other orders as if they were locked For example if order number 150 is currently locked So all orders after this number are also considered locked below is my code : ------------------------------------------------ var currentDate = Context.GetCurrentGlobalDateTime(); var currentDate1 = currentDate.ConvertToLocalNormalisedDateTime(); var SalesOrders = SalesOrder.QueryByElements; var SalesOrderDate_SelParams = SalesOrders.CreateSelectionParams(); SalesOrderDate_SelParams.Add(SalesOrders.Status.ItemListCustomerOrderLifeCycleStatusCode, "I", "EQ", "1"); SalesOrderDate_SelParams.Add(SalesOrders.Status.ItemListCustomerOrderLifeCycleStatusCode, "I", "EQ", "2"); var SalesOrderData_Results = SalesOrders.Execute(SalesOrderDate_SelParams); var SalesOrderData_ResultsCopy = SalesOrders.Execute(SalesOrderDate_SelParams); foreach (var SO in SalesOrderData_ResultsCopy) { var uuid; uuid = Library::UUID.ParseFromString(SO.UUID.content.ToString());// sales order id var messages; var lock; lock = BOAction.CheckLock("SalesOrder", "http://sap.com/xi/AP/CRM/Global", "Root", uuid); messages = lock.MessageTypeItem; var validationFailed : Common:Indicator=false; var a; if (!lock.IsInitial() && messages.Count() > 0) { foreach (var message in messages) { if (message.MessageSeverityText == "E" && message.MessageID.content == "CL_CDA_DOCHD/000") { validationFailed = true; a = SalesOrderData_Results.Remove(ins => ins.ID.content == SO.ID.content); SalesOrderData_Results = a; lock.Clear(); } } messages.Clear(); } } ------------------------------------------------ I mean, actually my code is not completely correct and I probably don't check it in the right way Anyone who can help me, I would be very happy thanks

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Internal table to binary for EXCEL
entry.content:
<html>

TRY.
        cl_salv_table=>factory(                               "#EC CI_NOORDER
          IMPORTING
            r_salv_table = DATA(r_salv)                          " Basis Class Simple ALV Tables
          CHANGING
            t_table      = t_intab
        ).
      CATCH cx_salv_msg. " ALV: General Error Class with Message
    ENDTRY.
    TRY.
        DATA(it_fcat) = cl_salv_controller_metadata=>get_lvc_fieldcatalog(
          EXPORTING
            r_columns      = r_salv->get_columns( )                 " ALV Filter
            r_aggregations = r_salv->get_aggregations( ) ).
      CATCH cx_salv_msg.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.
    READ TABLE it_fcat ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_fcat>) WITH KEY fieldname = 'PO_LIFNR'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      <fs_fcat>-reptext = 'PO Supplier'(003).
    ENDIF.
    TRY.
        cl_salv_bs_lex=>export_from_result_data_table(
          EXPORTING
            is_format            = if_salv_bs_lex_format=>mc_format_xlsx
            ir_result_data_table =  cl_salv_ex_util=>factory_result_data_table(
          EXPORTING
            r_data              =  r_data
            t_fieldcatalog      = it_fcat )
          IMPORTING
            er_result_file       = DATA(er_result_file) ).
      CATCH cx_salv_unexpected_param_value. " Unexpected value in method parameter
    ENDTRY.
*  t_btab = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_solix( er_result_file ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
      EXPORTING
        buffer        = er_result_file
      IMPORTING
        output_length = len
      TABLES
        binary_tab    = t_btab.

</html>

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA XS Advanced Application - Node.js service Frequently crashed
entry.content:
Dear Gregorw,

As per your suggestion, We are change the node version "node": "12.x". Still we are facing same issue.

9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   # Fatal error in , line 0
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   # Check failed: IsEmpty().
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #FailureMessage Object: 0x7fff94e9e070

entry.title: Re: Datasphere performance issue
entry.content:
Hi, 

# SAC crashes - OoM?

# In DSP you can get PlanViz file, which you can analyze in Studio. Could help, but not for the SAC Crash.

# For SAC there is the the Performance Analysis Tool. But before you deep dive there, go to DSP. The 40 seconds runtime is quite a lot for the 20M entries. First you need to get the runtime in DSP down - can bypass by persisting the view and consuming in SAC via analytic model.

I would: 

1. lower the complexity. Remove the calculations in step#6 / remove the step #6 . Check the runtime. I assume the calculations between step #2 and #3 are necessary for the union in stepp #3, if not, remove them as well.  Check if you go for runtime < 10 seconds. 

2. persist this view. In the same task chain, where you have the COPA source tables, add step to persist this view and then re-use it in analytic model, which you consume in SAC. 

3. How do you connect DSP to SAC? What do you consume? Try analytic model -  Worked for me the best way. 

Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Remote Table dynamic filter pushdown
entry.content:
Hi @RG20 ,

sadly i am not sure. Maybe you can test some variances like different data types or expressions to push down.
BR,

Thore

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow : Tables not getting imported using...
entry.content: Please check SU53 logs in the source system for the RFC user that is used to connect from ECC to Datasphere for any authorization issue. You can also apply ST05 or ST01 trace for that RFC user and perform operation if nothing is seen in SU53.

Thank you in advance!

Regards,

Aneta

Antonia

entry.title: project system objects
entry.content:

a) Standard WBS element

b) Standard network activity

c) Operative WBS element 

d) Operational network activity

entry.title: Re: Datasphere performance issue
entry.content: Thanks for your reply. I've analyzed the PlanViz file and change the model and now it's down to 2 secs in the preview of analytical model. But when it's directly consumed by SAC, it takes almost 2 mins to bring out the result. Guess i have to work with the analysis tools with SAC

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP

Re: CSS Designs for a Stylish and Optimized SAP Analytics Cloud Experience

entry.title: Re: Enhanced RISE with SAP Methodology with clean core quality checks
entry.content:
Hi, for  Conduct Clean Core Quality Gate 4

the template is still missing. Any update on that? 

BR,

Mark

entry.title: Best practices for property exchange using ProcessDirect Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Cloud Integration experts,

I’m currently working on decomposing a large integration flow into smaller, modular flows that communicate via the ProcessDirect adapter. My goal is to make the consumer integration flows as dynamic as possible for reusability purposes. Given that the ProcessDirect adapter doesn’t support property propagation, I’m looking for best practices to pass properties between iFlows.

One option I’m considering is passing the properties as message headers, though I doubt that using headers for this is ideal. Another option is temporarily storing the properties in a datastore and using a correlation ID to retrieve them in the target flow, but would require some additional steps.

Is there a simpler or more efficient approach? Any recommendations or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: How to install DTW on client/workstation PC's with SAP Business One 10.0
entry.content:
We would like to install DTW on our client/workstation PCs so that our users can upload required information to system with DTW by themselves. Would anyone guide how to install DTW in client PC? Can user connect DTW with their individual credentials instead of 'MANAGER' credential?

We are using "SAP Business One 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.201) FP 2208"

entry.title: HCM integrates with which other functional modules like MM,PP,SD,ETC...?And in which senerios?
entry.content: HCM is integrate with officially FICO but what about others! Try to explain with example.

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Process Started but automation not triggered
entry.content: So I need a very expensive agent to run some REST calls because the normal actions do not allow to use expressions for input fields? Thats horrible. I just wanted to use 4 REST calls with the input of each other but because it is OData I need to change the filter to something with a static string I define and the variable I get from the previous call and it's not possible with actions. I can only do this in an automation. And for that I need an agent then that is, as I said, very expensive?

entry.title: Re: CAP CDS annotation for direct date picker
entry.content:
Hello @amallari,

In CAP, there is no annotation for Date fields like we do in ABAP CDS.

Please use annotation Capabilities on filter to display the DatePicker:

annotate YOUR_ENTITY with @Capabilities : {
    FilterRestrictions : {
        $Type              : 'Capabilities.FilterRestrictionsType',
        RequiredProperties : [ YOUR_PROPERTY ],
        FilterExpressionRestrictions : [
            {
                $Type : 'Capabilities.FilterExpressionRestrictionType',
                Property : YOUR_PROPERTY,
                AllowedExpressions : 'SingleRange' (please see documentation for other values)
            }
        ]
    }
};

Regards,
Sébastien

entry.title: Docusig CLM integration Ariba Contracts
entry.content:
I'm trying to set up the integration between Ariba Contracts and Docusign CLM but in Ariba I need to add some specific information; Specifies the URL context that is being integrated with SAP Ariba Contracts and Specifies the hostname that is being integrated with SAP Ariba Contracts.  I've looking informtacion about wich information the system needs but no luck so far, Does any body have an idea wich information is needed?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Einvoicing for India
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing Electronic Customer Invoices for India. Can someone please explain what is the exact difference between the Einvoice for Customers Legacy (SAP Cloud Integration or SAP Integration Suite ) and the new Einvoicing for customers (SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition). We need to understand if there is any difference besides the way to push to Tax authority/Government

Please find the Sap help portal links below;

Einvoice for Customers Legacy

Electronic Invoices (Legacy) | SAP Help Portal

Electronic Customer Invoice

Electronic Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Market Segments Apps showing unusual fiscal periods
entry.content: Hello @Susanne_Helbig, thank you for your input. Since these fiscal year variants are not used by the customer but shown i can however recommend to ignore them. TZhank oyu for your answer.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for property exchange using ProcessDirect Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content: For propagation of properties the headers would be your go to. The option of datastore is a LOT of overhead for managing exchange of properties. When appropriate use properties and when necessary go with headers... that would be my advice.

entry.title: Re: MDG BP search result list enhancement
entry.content:
Please check this document

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/1009ddb7-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

entry.title: Re: Internal table to binary for EXCEL
entry.content: What is your issue, what is your question?

entry.title: Re: What SPs in BO 4.3 still support UNV?
entry.content: Per the Statement of Direction and this blog, unv universes will not be deprecated until BusinessObjects 2025, so it's safe to say that they will be supported through the end of BI4.3.

entry.title: Re: Sap Business one SSO Not working when switching Database
entry.content:
Hello Andy,

We are facing similar issue have you found any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
Dear @Sandra_Rossi,
In my actual program I am not using GUI_UPLOAD, the code I send was my testing code, but the problem is the same. This is the code from my real program for saving file: 

IF lt_tab[] IS NOT INITIAL.
    OPEN DATASET lv_filename FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
    LOOP AT lt_tab INTO ls_tab.
      TRANSFER ls_tab TO lv_filename.
    ENDLOOP.
    CLOSE DATASET lv_filename.
    REFRESH lt_tab.
  ENDIF.

However, I did set trunc_trailing_blanks to false in GUI_DOWNLOAD. The result is the same. 
Do you have any other ideas how could I solve this?
And thank you so much for answering.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA XS Advanced Application - Node.js service Frequently crashed
entry.content: Please check https://nodejs.org/en/about/previous-releases for what is the supported version. You would need to update the buildpack on your HANA XSA of couse also.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere performance issue
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/troubleshooting-of-performance-issues-between-sap-analytics-cloud-sac-and/ba-p/13801417

entry.title: Re: Introducing Dark CPI web extension For SAP Applications
entry.content:
We've finally got a "native" dark mode for SAP Cloud Integration.

Great work! 😀

entry.title: Re: Learning Journey Path
entry.content:
Thanks for writing @Salish_Kumar ! an invite is on its way. 

How did you end up finding us?

entry.title: Re: Our Way into the Clean Core
entry.content: This is a very interesting read especially since it covers the whole process that most SAP customers and partners go through when dealing with clean core ambitions on one hand and legacy developments  on the other.

entry.title: Re: Choosing the topic for Free certification attempt
entry.content:
Hi @toukta_sylavy ,

the exam attempt works for any exam!

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Certification
entry.content:
Hi @Moeder ,

thanks for sharing a bit about yourself. An invite is on it's way to you.

Please keep me updated on your progress. I'd love to hear about a future promotion for you. 🙂

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content: If you want to stay with SAP Standard you create a custom UI5 Application using the sap.ndc.BarcodeScannerButton control. The ABAP backend needs to provide the OData API that then processes the scaned code.

entry.title: Re: QC result recording after UD in SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you select the option "with long-term inspection" on the selection screen?

entry.title: Re: Free-Hand SQL Rights missing in 4.2 SAP BI
entry.content: I've seen similar behavior after an upgrade - missing rights in the Applications tab in CMC.  I can't find the SAP Note right now, but from what I remember there were two solutions given.  The first is just to run a "Repair" installation.  If that doesn't work, then there's a process to manually copy .dfo files from one place to another.  For us, the second solution did the trick.

entry.title: Re: Seeking Guidance on Choosing the Right Learning Path for SAP Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hi @HussainSiddiq ,

Thanks for writing. The first step is always to scan job openings to understand what is in demand for your local market.

With that said, SAP Finance skills are usually a safe bet as they combine two rather complex skill sets. Please read more here and tag Stefanos as he is our resident finance expert.

Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: Hello!
entry.content: Welcome aboard @moh_hafez1 !

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content:
The code I have shared is if you can derive the fields/structure of the dynamic table during runtime. In the blog link that you have shared, the declaration of lt_comp is there.  But for the code snippet I have shared, I used a structure from the ABAP type  group - DATA lt_comp TYPE abap_component_tab therefore field symbol type is abap_component_tab as well, which has fields NAME and TYPE to store the fieldname and its corresponding data type of the dynamic table that will be built.  

I hope this is clear enough.

entry.title: Issue with Purchase Order Document Type Defaulting
entry.content:
Hi 

We've recently created new document types: ZQYS for purchase requisitions and ZQYS for purchase orders.

Problem: When creating a purchase order (PO) referencing a purchase requisition (PR) with document type ZQYS, the system is defaulting to the document type NB. We have to manually change the PO document type to ZQYS. Request: How can we configure the system to automatically set the purchase order document type to ZQYS when the referenced purchase requisition is also ZQYS? 

Thank you for your assistance.  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Purchase Order Document Type Defaulting
entry.content:
Hello,

You can refer to the below help link:

Determining Document Types for Purchase Orders Created from Purchase Requisition Items

Thank you

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content:
If you can determine the field name or reference during runtime:

For data element, lv_fieldstr = 'MATNR'.

For table field reference, lv_fieldstr = 'MARA-MATNR'.

entry.title: Re: Move GOS items from SOFFCONT1 to Content Server using RSIRPIRL with parameter "delete later
entry.content:
Please check note: 2991944 - Introducing the Delay mode in report RSIRPIRL

 

Symptom

You would like to execute report RSIRPIRL in delay Mode. In this new delay mode, your content deletion from the source repository will not be deleted immediately but it can happen at later point.
Also, if the documents are stored with file size as empty you would like to eliminate migration of these documents.

Reason and Prerequisites

We have found that sometimes there are issues with creating the documents in the target repository. If the documents are deleted from the source the retrieval becomes difficult.  This feature will help you with such issues.
If you have saved the documents without file size in File tables, after migrating those documents to the HTTP content server, the documents contents will not be saved on HTTP content server. This will result in data loss.

Solution

This Note delivers a new feature.

  • P_UPDATE - Enable the checkbox to avoid deletion of the documents from source repository immediately.
    System will mark the documents for deletion. Later when executing Report RSIR_CONTENT_UNMARK_PRELIM after 30 days, the documents from the actual source repository get deleted.
    • P_EMPTY - Enable the checkbox to avoid migration of documents between repositories with file size in File tables as '0'.

      Please Note: Please avoid executing the report with option 'Delete Later' (P_UPDATE = 'X') multiple times using the same date range as it will create inconsistencies in the system and may lead to data loss.

entry.title: Re: Usage of filter on multilevel expand
entry.content:
Assuming 2) returns all headers you want, and you only want to have items with material '123', you can extend the query with a nested filter on items:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsales_order_srv/srvd_a2x/sap/zsales_order/0001/SalesOrderHeader?$expand=_Item($filter=Material eq '123';$expand=_Schedule)&$filter=(_Item/any(s: s/Material eq '123')

entry.title: Re: Custom fields are not appearing in Sales reports
entry.content: For Sales Volume - Flexible Analysis - what is the actual Queries/View used? I don't see anything clearly how to enable a custom field for this app.

entry.title: Domain Assignment Through Connectors
entry.content:
Hi,

We would like to assign users to a domain by updating the Connectors section in:

Learning Admin > System Admin > Configuration > CONNECTORS

We tried adding users to one of two existing domains based on their organization ID using the code:

sfuser.field.transform.data.DMN_ID.DOMAIN1\ Population=Org1 (05)

sfuser.field.transform.data.DMN_ID.DOMAIN2\ Population=Org2 (80), Org3 (1), Glanbia Org4 (90)

Result:

The system created 2 new domains, which were copies of our 2 existing domains with the word 'population' appended

Domain 1 Population

Domain 2 Population

Question

Is there a way to assign users to the existing domains rather than have the system create new ones.

 

Thank you

Kind regards,

Susan

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content: Thanks for the introductions everyone and sorry for the delay!

entry.title: Re: B1iWS Connector - B1iServerURL does not update with n...
entry.content: Hi Nidhi

entry.title: Self-introduction
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano

I’m Trang Nguyen, I have a diverse background in supply chain management, software development, and technology consulting. My professional journey has led me to explore the transformative capabilities of SAP, and I’m eager to deepen my expertise in this dynamic field.

With several years of experience in supply chain roles and a solid foundation in IT business analysis, I’ve developed a strong interest in how SAP can drive digital transformation and operational efficiency. My goal is to leverage SAP’s powerful tools to enhance business processes and contribute to innovative solutions within the digital landscape.

Through this thread, I look forward to engaging with fellow professionals, sharing insights, and exploring SAP’s digital initiatives. I’m excited to be part of this community and to learn from the wealth of knowledge here.

Best regards,
Trang Nguyen
